mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where unemployment rises above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive quarterly returns in greater than 55% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and short-term earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth correlates with negative 5-day returns in greater than 60% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate compresses real yields and signals delayed monetary tightening, prompting equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 58% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and wage-driven consumption, mechanically supporting corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive quarterly returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below the 0.5% threshold signals demand contraction and recession risk, triggering defensive portfolio rebalancing that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below the threshold is followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 62% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total employment confirm synchronized supply and labor market strength, generating short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 5-day returns in greater than 55% of triggered instances.
